---

## Key Sub-Types — Detailed

### SettingDocument
Bound in each entity's \\\`BindSetting.documents\\\`. This is the **core mechanism for feeding narrative knowledge to the AI** during gameplay.
\\\`\\\`\\\`typescript
{
  name: string,              // Document title (unique within an entity's documents)
  content: string,           // Markdown content — the actual setting/lore text the AI reads
  static_priority?: number,  // If set, document is ALWAYS recalled (higher = earlier in context). Use for essential lore.
  condition?: string,        // Natural language condition for RAG recall (e.g. "When this character appears", "During combat")
  disable?: boolean          // If true, document is temporarily hidden from recall (useful for toggling without deleting)
}
\\\`\\\`\\\`
**Rules:**
- Must have at least one of \\\`static_priority\\\` or \\\`condition\\\` — otherwise the document is never recalled
- \\\`static_priority\\\` = always on, good for: world background, player character bio
- \\\`condition\\\` = on-demand, good for: NPC details, situational rules, growth phase docs
- Don't overuse \\\`static_priority\\\` — too many always-on docs inflate the AI context and reduce quality
- \\\`condition\\\` is a **natural language description** that the LLM evaluates at runtime to decide relevance. Write it as a clear trigger: \\\`"When the player enters the Dark Forest"\\\`, \\\`"When affinity with this character exceeds 50"\\\`

### StatusEffect
Temporary or persistent effects on an entity.
\\\`\\\`\\\`typescript
{
  instance_id: string,       // Unique ID for this effect instance
  display_name?: string,     // **UI-facing**: shown to the player in the game interface
  remark?: string,           // **AI-facing**: helps the AI understand the effect's narrative meaning, source, and implications
  data?: any,                // Arbitrary structured data (e.g. { damage: 2, duration: 5 })
  add_at?: string,           // System-managed timestamp, do NOT set manually
  last_update_at?: string    // System-managed timestamp, do NOT set manually
}
\\\`\\\`\\\`
**Key distinction**: \\\`display_name\\\` is what the player sees (e.g. "Poisoned"); \\\`remark\\\` is what the AI reads to understand context (e.g. "Bitten by cave spider, loses 2 HP per turn, curable with antidote herb").

### Item
Items in a character's \\\`Inventory.items\\\`. No global item registry — items only exist when held.
\\\`\\\`\\\`typescript
{
  id: string,                // Unique item ID
  count: number,             // Stack count
  name: string,              // Display name
  description: string,       // Short description
  details: string[],         // Additional detail lines (e.g. ["Sharp blade", "Glows faintly in the dark"])
  equipped?: boolean         // Whether the item is currently equipped/worn/active (UI shows equipped state)
}
\\\`\\\`\\\`
World-level items (shop catalogs, discoverable loot) should be described in SettingDocuments on World or Region.

### CustomComponentDef
Registered in \\\`World.CustomComponentRegistry\\\`:
\\\`\\\`\\\`typescript
{
  component_key: string,     // Unique key used to mount data on entities
  component_name: string,    // Display name
  is_array: boolean,         // Whether the data is an array (e.g. party members) or a single object
  type_schema?: object,      // Optional JSON Schema describing the data structure
  data_registry?: Array<{item_id: string, data: any}>  // Optional preset data catalog (e.g. predefined skill/spell list that entities can reference)
}
\\\`\\\`\\\`
\\\`data_registry\\\` is a **lookup table** of preset items for this component type. For example, a "spells" component might register all available spells in \\\`data_registry\\\`, and each character's mounted data references spell IDs from this list.

---

## Cross-Entity Data Dependencies (IMPORTANT)

Several data fields are **definitions vs instances** — the schema/definition lives on World, while each entity only holds values or references. Modifying one side often requires syncing the other.

| Definition (World) | Instance (Entity) | Relationship |
|---|---|---|
| \\\`World.Registry.creature_attr_fields[]\\\` | \\\`Creature.attrs\\\` (Record<string, number\\|string>) | attr_fields defines **which keys** exist; each creature's attrs only holds **values**. Adding/removing a field must sync all creatures. |
| \\\`World.CustomComponentRegistry.custom_components[]\\\` | \\\`Creature.CustomComponents.custom_components[]\\\` | Registry defines component_key + schema; entities mount data by component_key. Registering a new component must also mount data on relevant entities. |
| \\\`Organizations[].Organization.organization_id\\\` | \\\`Creature.organization_id\\\` | Creature references an org by ID. Renaming/deleting an org must update all creatures that reference it. |
| \\\`Regions[].Region.region_id\\\` + \\\`locations[].id\\\` | \\\`Creature.LocationRef { region_id, location_id }\\\` | Creature's location references a region+location. Deleting a location must update creatures there. |
| \\\`Creatures[].Creature.creature_id\\\` | \\\`Relationship.target_creature_id\\\` | Relationships reference other creatures by ID. Deleting a creature must clean up all relationships pointing to it. |
| (No item registry) | \\\`Creature.Inventory.items[]\\\` | Items only exist when held by a character. World-level items go in SettingDocuments on World or Region. |

**Golden Rule**: When modifying a definition (e.g. adding an attr field, renaming an org), always check and sync all entities that reference it.
`

/**
 * Built-in Skill: Workflow
 * Fine-tuning workflow for post-WorldBuilder data refinement.
 */
export const BUILTIN_WORKFLOW =
`# Fine-Tuning Workflow

## Your Role
You are a post-WorldBuilder fine-tuning assistant. The game world has already been generated.
Your job: help users **modify, adjust, supplement, and polish** existing data.

## Processing Flow

1. **Check current state** — \\\`get_state_overview()\\\` to understand what exists
2. **Check memories** — \\\`list_memories()\\\` for previous context
3. **Locate target** — \\\`get_state_content(path)\\\` to inspect the specific data to modify
4. **Read relevant Skills** — Code recipes → \\\`builtin_editing_recipes\\\`, advanced → \\\`builtin_advanced_tuning\\\` (StateData schema is already in the system prompt)
5. **Confirm plan** — Tell the user what you'll change, wait for confirmation
6. **Execute** — \\\`update_state_with_javascript(code)\\\` to apply changes
7. **Validate** — \\\`check_state_error()\\\` to verify consistency
8. **Record** — \\\`save_memory()\\\` to log what was done

---

## Key Principles

- **Check before modifying** — Always \\\`get_state_content\\\` to see current values first
- **Minimal changes** — Only change what the user asked for, don't "optimize" other data
- **Confirm destructive ops** — Ask before deleting characters, clearing documents, bulk rewrites
- **Use array index carefully** — Characters/Regions/Orgs are in arrays; confirm index with \\\`get_state_overview\\\`
- **IDs must be unique** — Use meaningful IDs (e.g., \\\`"npc_tavern_keeper"\\\`)
- **References must be valid** — organization_id, region_id, target_creature_id must point to existing entities

---

## Common Fine-Tuning Scenarios

| User Request | Steps |
|---|---|
| Modify a character's attributes | get_state_content("Creatures") → find index → update attrs |
| Give item to a character | find character index → push to Inventory.items |
| Make an item exist in the world | add SettingDocument to World or Region BindSetting (no item registry) |
| Add a new NPC | read Schema → push new entity to Creatures array |
| Change relationship between characters | update both sides of Relationship component |
| Edit a setting document | locate BindSetting.documents → find by name → update content |
| Adjust world time | modify World.GameTime |
| Add a custom component | register in CustomComponentRegistry → mount on entities |
| Rewrite opening story | modify GameInitialStory.background / start_story |

> For code examples of each operation, read \\\`builtin_editing_recipes\\\`.
`

/**
 * Built-in Skill: Editing Recipes
 * Concrete code examples for common editing operations on existing StateData.
 */
export const BUILTIN_EDITING_RECIPES =
`# Editing Recipes — Common Modification Operations

## Character Modifications

### Modify Character Basic Info
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// First use get_state_content("Creatures") to find the target character index
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "target_id");
state.Creatures[idx].Creature.name = "New Name";
state.Creatures[idx].Creature.gender = "Female";
state.Creatures[idx].Creature.appearance = { body: "Appearance description", clothing: "Clothing description" };
state.Creatures[idx].Creature.goal = "Character's current goal";
state.Creatures[idx].Creature.emotion = "Calm";
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Modify Character Attribute Values
> **Key**: Attribute **field definitions** live in \\\`World.Registry.creature_attr_fields\\\`; each creature's \\\`attrs\\\` only holds **values** for those fields. Adding a new attribute requires both sides.
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Modify existing attribute value
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "target_id");
state.Creatures[idx].Creature.attrs.strength = 15;
state.Creatures[idx].Creature.attrs.intelligence = 20;

// To add a NEW attribute — must register field definition first, then set value on all creatures
state.World.Registry.creature_attr_fields.push({
  field_name: "luck", hint: "Character luck value", field_display_name: "Luck"
});
state.Creatures.forEach(c => { if (c.Creature) c.Creature.attrs.luck = 10; });
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Add/Modify Inventory Items (Character Possession)
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "target_id");
if (!state.Creatures[idx].Inventory) state.Creatures[idx].Inventory = { items: [] };
// Add new item
state.Creatures[idx].Inventory.items.push({
  id: "item_id", count: 1, name: "Item Name", description: "Description", details: []
});
// Modify existing item
const itemIdx = state.Creatures[idx].Inventory.items.findIndex(i => i.id === "item_id");
if (itemIdx >= 0) state.Creatures[idx].Inventory.items[itemIdx].count = 5;
// Remove item
state.Creatures[idx].Inventory.items = state.Creatures[idx].Inventory.items.filter(i => i.id !== "item_id");
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Make an Item Exist in the World (NOT in anyone's inventory)
There is **no global item registry**. \\\`Inventory.items\\\` only stores items a character **currently possesses**.
If the user wants an item to **exist in the world** (shop sells it, treasure chest contains it, loot drop, etc.),
add it as a **SettingDocument** on the World or the relevant Region:
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Item exists at a shop in a region
const rIdx = state.Regions.findIndex(r => r.Region?.region_id === "town_center");
if (!state.Regions[rIdx].BindSetting) state.Regions[rIdx].BindSetting = { documents: [] };
state.Regions[rIdx].BindSetting.documents.push({
  name: "Blacksmith Shop Inventory",
  content: "## Available Items\\n- **Iron Sword** (50 gold): A sturdy blade forged by the local smith\\n- **Leather Shield** (30 gold): Basic protection",
  condition: "When the player visits the blacksmith shop"
});

// Item exists as world-level knowledge (e.g. legendary artifact)
if (!state.World.BindSetting) state.World.BindSetting = { documents: [] };
state.World.BindSetting.documents.push({
  name: "Legendary Artifacts",
  content: "## The Sword of Dawn\\nA mythical weapon said to be hidden in the Northern Ruins...",
  condition: "When legendary weapons or the Sword of Dawn is mentioned"
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Modify Character Relationships
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Relationships are usually bidirectional — update both sides
const idxA = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "alice");
const idxB = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "bob");

// Ensure Relationship component exists
if (!state.Creatures[idxA].Relationship) state.Creatures[idxA].Relationship = { relationships: [] };
if (!state.Creatures[idxB].Relationship) state.Creatures[idxB].Relationship = { relationships: [] };

// A → B: update or create
const relA = state.Creatures[idxA].Relationship.relationships.find(r => r.target_creature_id === "bob");
if (relA) { relA.name = "Close Friend"; relA.value = 80; }
else { state.Creatures[idxA].Relationship.relationships.push({ target_creature_id: "bob", name: "Close Friend", value: 80 }); }

// B → A: same pattern
const relB = state.Creatures[idxB].Relationship.relationships.find(r => r.target_creature_id === "alice");
if (relB) { relB.name = "Close Friend"; relB.value = 80; }
else { state.Creatures[idxB].Relationship.relationships.push({ target_creature_id: "alice", name: "Close Friend", value: 80 }); }
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Add/Modify Status Effects
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "target_id");
if (!state.Creatures[idx].StatusEffects) state.Creatures[idx].StatusEffects = { status_effects: [] };
// Add
state.Creatures[idx].StatusEffects.status_effects.push({
  instance_id: "effect_id", display_name: "Poisoned", remark: "Bitten by a venomous snake",
  data: { damage: 2, duration: 5 }
});
// Modify existing
const eff = state.Creatures[idx].StatusEffects.status_effects.find(e => e.instance_id === "effect_id");
if (eff) { eff.data.duration = 10; eff.remark = "Worsened"; }
// Remove
state.Creatures[idx].StatusEffects.status_effects = state.Creatures[idx].StatusEffects.status_effects.filter(e => e.instance_id !== "effect_id");
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Add a New NPC
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
state.Creatures.push({
  entity_id: 0, // System auto-assigns
  Creature: {
    creature_id: "npc_unique_id", // Must be unique
    name: "Character Name",
    titles: [],
    appearance: { body: "Appearance", clothing: "Clothing" },
    gender: "Male",
    attrs: { /* Use fields defined in World.Registry.creature_attr_fields */ },
    known_infos: [],
    goal: "Character's goal"
  }
  // Do NOT add IsPlayer — NPCs don't need it
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Delete a Character
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
const targetId = "target_id";
state.Creatures = state.Creatures.filter(c => c.Creature?.creature_id !== targetId);
// Also clean up relationship references from other characters
state.Creatures.forEach(c => {
  if (c.Relationship) {
    c.Relationship.relationships = c.Relationship.relationships.filter(
      r => r.target_creature_id !== targetId
    );
  }
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

---

## Setting Document Modifications

### Edit an Entity's Setting Document
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Character setting document
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "target_id");
const doc = state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting?.documents?.find(d => d.name === "Biography");
if (doc) doc.content = "New document content...";

// World setting document
const worldDoc = state.World.BindSetting?.documents?.find(d => d.name === "World Background");
if (worldDoc) worldDoc.content = "New world background...";

// Region setting document
const rIdx = state.Regions.findIndex(r => r.Region?.region_id === "region_id");
const rDoc = state.Regions[rIdx].BindSetting?.documents?.find(d => d.name === "Region Overview");
if (rDoc) rDoc.content = "New region overview...";
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Add a New Setting Document
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Add to character
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "target_id");
if (!state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting) state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting = { documents: [] };
state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting.documents.push({
  name: "Secret Past",
  content: "Document content...",
  condition: "When the character's past is mentioned"  // OR use static_priority: 50 for always-on
});

// Add to world
if (!state.World.BindSetting) state.World.BindSetting = { documents: [] };
state.World.BindSetting.documents.push({
  name: "Combat Rules",
  content: "Rules content...",
  condition: "When combat occurs"
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Delete a Setting Document
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// From character
state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting.documents =
  state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting.documents.filter(d => d.name !== "Document to Delete");
// From world
state.World.BindSetting.documents =
  state.World.BindSetting.documents.filter(d => d.name !== "Document to Delete");
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Setting Document Priority & Condition Guide
- \\\`static_priority\\\`: Numeric, always recalled (use for: world background, player character bio)
- \\\`condition\\\`: Natural language, recalled on demand (use for: NPC settings, situational rules)
- Avoid overusing static_priority — too many always-on docs inflate context

---

## Region & Organization Modifications

### Modify Region Info
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
const idx = state.Regions.findIndex(r => r.Region?.region_id === "target_region");
state.Regions[idx].Region.region_name = "New Region Name";
state.Regions[idx].Region.description = "New description";
// Add a location
state.Regions[idx].Region.locations.push({ id: "loc_id", name: "Location Name", description: "Description" });
// Add a path between locations
state.Regions[idx].Region.paths.push({
  src_region: "target_region", src_location: "loc_a",
  to_region: "target_region", to_location: "loc_b",
  discovered: true, description: "A winding path through the forest"
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Modify Organization Info
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
const idx = state.Organizations.findIndex(o => o.Organization?.organization_id === "target_org");
state.Organizations[idx].Organization.name = "New Org Name";
state.Organizations[idx].Organization.description = "New description";
// Add territory
state.Organizations[idx].Organization.territories.push({ region_id: "region_id", location_id: "loc_id" });
\\\`\\\`\\\`

---

## World-Level Modifications

### Modify Game Time
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
state.World.GameTime = { year: 2028, month: 3, day: 15, hour: 8, minute: 0 };
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Modify Attribute Field Definitions
> **Cross-entity sync required!** Field definitions in World ↔ values in each Creature.attrs
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// ADD a new field — register definition + set initial value on all creatures
state.World.Registry.creature_attr_fields.push({
  field_name: "luck", hint: "Character's luck value", field_display_name: "Luck"
});
state.Creatures.forEach(c => { if (c.Creature) c.Creature.attrs.luck = 10; });

// REMOVE a field — delete definition + clean up all creatures
state.World.Registry.creature_attr_fields = state.World.Registry.creature_attr_fields.filter(f => f.field_name !== "luck");
state.Creatures.forEach(c => { if (c.Creature) delete c.Creature.attrs.luck; });

// RENAME a field — update definition + migrate all creature values
const field = state.World.Registry.creature_attr_fields.find(f => f.field_name === "old_name");
if (field) field.field_name = "new_name";
state.Creatures.forEach(c => {
  if (c.Creature && c.Creature.attrs.old_name !== undefined) {
    c.Creature.attrs.new_name = c.Creature.attrs.old_name;
    delete c.Creature.attrs.old_name;
  }
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Modify Opening Story
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
state.GameInitialStory = {
  background: "New background story...",
  start_story: "New opening narrative..."
};
// Or modify just one field:
state.GameInitialStory.background = "Updated background...";
\\\`\\\`\\\`
`

/**
 * Built-in Skill: Advanced Tuning
 * Advanced operations: CustomComponent, condition-driven documents, batch operations.
 */
export const BUILTIN_ADVANCED_TUNING =
`# Advanced Tuning Guide

## CustomComponent Operations

### Modify Existing CustomComponent Data
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "target_id");
const comp = state.Creatures[idx].CustomComponents?.custom_components?.find(
  cc => cc.component_key === "cultivation_state"
);
if (comp) comp.data.realm = "Golden Core";
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Register a New CustomComponent
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// 1. Register definition in World
if (!state.World.CustomComponentRegistry) state.World.CustomComponentRegistry = { custom_components: [] };
state.World.CustomComponentRegistry.custom_components.push({
  component_key: "reputation",
  component_name: "Reputation System",
  is_array: false,
  type_schema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: { fame: { type: "integer" }, infamy: { type: "integer" } }
  }
});

// 2. Mount on target characters
state.Creatures.forEach(c => {
  if (!c.CustomComponents) c.CustomComponents = { custom_components: [] };
  c.CustomComponents.custom_components.push({
    component_key: "reputation", data: { fame: 0, infamy: 0 }
  });
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Remove a CustomComponent
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Remove from all creatures
state.Creatures.forEach(c => {
  if (c.CustomComponents) {
    c.CustomComponents.custom_components = c.CustomComponents.custom_components.filter(
      cc => cc.component_key !== "reputation"
    );
  }
});
// Remove definition from registry
state.World.CustomComponentRegistry.custom_components =
  state.World.CustomComponentRegistry.custom_components.filter(d => d.component_key !== "reputation");
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Selection Guide: When to Use What

| Scenario | Use | Why |
|----------|-----|-----|
| Persistent core data (level, money, faction) | CustomComponent | Structured, typed, registered |
| Temporary effects (buffs, poison, weather) | StatusEffect | Has display_name for UI, remark for AI |
| Interpersonal relations, affinity | Relationship | Built-in numeric + name system |

---

## Condition-Driven Setting Documents

### Phase-Based Character Documents
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Dynamic personality based on affinity value
const idx = state.Creatures.findIndex(c => c.Creature?.creature_id === "npc_id");
if (!state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting) state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting = { documents: [] };
state.Creatures[idx].BindSetting.documents.push(
  { name: "Early Phase", condition: "When affinity < 30", content: "Cold and distant, rarely speaks..." },
  { name: "Mid Phase", condition: "When affinity is between 30 and 70", content: "Gradually opening up..." },
  { name: "Late Phase", condition: "When affinity > 70", content: "Fully trusting, shares secrets..." }
);
\\\`\\\`\\\`

> **Core pattern**: CustomComponent/StatusEffect/Relationship store numerical values → SettingDocument's condition references those values → AI dynamically selects the appropriate narrative style.

### Priority vs Condition

| Use Case | Approach |
|----------|----------|
| World background (always needed) | \\\`static_priority: 100\\\` |
| Player character bio (always needed) | \\\`static_priority: 80\\\` |
| NPC personality (only when present) | \\\`condition: "When this NPC appears"\\\` |
| Combat rules (only during combat) | \\\`condition: "During combat scenes"\\\` |
| Growth phases (based on values) | \\\`condition: "When affinity > 50"\\\` |

---

## Batch Operations

### Batch Adjust All Character Attributes
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Scale all strength values by 1.2x
state.Creatures.forEach(c => {
  if (c.Creature?.attrs?.strength) {
    c.Creature.attrs.strength = Math.round(c.Creature.attrs.strength * 1.2);
  }
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Batch Add Missing Components
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
state.Creatures.forEach(c => {
  if (!c.Inventory) c.Inventory = { items: [] };
  if (!c.Relationship) c.Relationship = { relationships: [] };
  if (!c.StatusEffects) c.StatusEffects = { status_effects: [] };
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

### Batch Update Setting Document Conditions
\\\`\\\`\\\`javascript
// Update all NPC documents to use a consistent condition pattern
state.Creatures.forEach(c => {
  if (!c.IsPlayer && c.BindSetting?.documents) {
    c.BindSetting.documents.forEach(doc => {
      if (!doc.static_priority && !doc.condition) {
        doc.condition = "When " + (c.Creature?.name || "this character") + " is present or mentioned";
      }
    });
  }
});
\\\`\\\`\\\`

---

## Data Consistency Checklist

After modifications, verify:
1. **ID uniqueness** — creature_id / region_id / organization_id must not repeat
2. **Reference validity** — organization_id references an existing org; LocationRef points to existing region+location
3. **Attribute consistency** — All creature attrs keys match World.Registry.creature_attr_fields definitions
4. **Relationship symmetry** — If A→B has a relationship, B→A usually should too
5. **Document balance** — Each entity should have at most 1-2 static_priority docs to avoid context bloat

> Use \\\`check_state_error()\\\` to automate most of these checks.
`

export function getBuiltinSkillContent(skillId: string): string | null {
  switch (skillId) {
    case 'builtin_editing_recipes': return BUILTIN_EDITING_RECIPES
    case 'builtin_workflow': return BUILTIN_WORKFLOW

  }

}

/**
 * SillyTavern Lorebook Parser
 *
 * Parses SillyTavern character card JSON files, extracting
 * character_book entries and card metadata.
 */

import type { LorebookData, LorebookEntry } from './worldBuilderNextTypes'

/**
 * Attempt to parse a SillyTavern character card JSON into LorebookData.
 * Returns null if the JSON is not a valid SillyTavern lorebook format.
 */
export function parseSillyTavernLorebook(
    json: unknown,
    filename: string
): LorebookData | null {
    if (!json || typeof json !== 'object') return null

    const root = json as Record<string, unknown>
    const data = root.data as Record<string, unknown> | undefined

    // Must have data.character_book.entries
    const characterBook = data?.character_book as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
    const rawEntries = characterBook?.entries
    if (!Array.isArray(rawEntries) || rawEntries.length === 0) return null

    const entries: LorebookEntry[] = []
    let totalChars = 0

    for (const raw of rawEntries) {
        if (!raw || typeof raw !== 'object') continue
        const e = raw as Record<string, unknown>
        const content = typeof e.content === 'string' ? e.content : ''
        entries.push({
            id: typeof e.id === 'number' ? e.id : entries.length,
            keys: Array.isArray(e.keys) ? e.keys.filter((k): k is string => typeof k === 'string') : [],
            comment: typeof e.comment === 'string' ? e.comment : '',
            content,
            enabled: e.enabled !== false,
        })
        totalChars += content.length
    }

    const str = (key: string) => {
        const v = data?.[key]
        return typeof v === 'string' ? v : ''
    }

    return {
        filename,
        name: str('name') || (root.name as string) || filename,
        description: str('description'),
        personality: str('personality'),
        scenario: str('scenario'),
        first_mes: str('first_mes'),
        mes_example: str('mes_example'),
        creator_notes: str('creator_notes'),
        entries,
        totalChars,
        uploadedAt: Date.now(),
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// Per-Phase Instructions (used as user messages in structured output calls)
// ============================================================================

const PHASE_INSTRUCTIONS: Record<WBNPhaseId, string> = {

    // ===== DRAFT =====
    synopsis: `
## Current Step: Draft

Based on the user's initial description and any reference material provided, generate a **lightweight draft** containing gameplay tone, opening timing, storyline, game mechanics, protagonist description, and name lists for regions/organizations/characters to be generated.

> The draft serves as directional guidance for subsequent steps and does not need to be a detailed planning document.

### Draft Requirements

Generate a draft containing the following 8 fields:

| Field | Description |
|-----|------|
| **tone** | Gameplay Tone — Overall atmosphere and narrative style (2-3 sentences) |
| **opening** | Opening Timing — At what point in time and what scene does the story begin (2-3 sentences) |
| **storyline** | Storyline — Main plot summary, core conflicts (3-5 sentences) |
| **mechanics** | Game Mechanics — Gameplay focus, core system overview (2-3 sentences) |
| **protagonist** | Protagonist Description — Identity, personality, initial state (2-3 sentences) |
| **creatures** | Character List — \`[{creature_id, name, is_player}]\` |
| **regions** | Region List — \`[{region_id, name}]\` |
| **organizations** | Organization List — \`[{organization_id, name}]\` (pass empty array if no organizations) |

**Draft field rules:**
- Text fields should be concise and clear, 2-5 sentences each
- Entity lists only need IDs and names
- **Character naming rule**: All creature_ids must correspond to a proper personal name (e.g., "Lin Xia", "Alice"), **strictly forbidden** to use identity/occupation as names
- Exactly one character's \`is_player\` should be true
`,

    // ===== WORLD DATA =====
    world_data: `
## Current Step: World Data

Based on the tone and game mechanics from the draft, generate world-level configuration data and setting documents.

### Temporal Anchoring Principle

The opening story was determined in the previous step; it is the temporal starting point of the entire game. All data you generate must reflect the world state **at the story's beginning**, not the state from later developments in the reference materials.

### Data to Generate

#### 1. creature_attr_fields — Character Attribute Field Definitions

> **Important**: creature_attr_fields are **universal attributes for all creatures in this world (including the player and all NPCs)**, not just for the player character. Design attribute fields that are meaningful for all characters.
> **creature_attr_fields must be intrinsic abilities or traits of a creature itself** (e.g., strength, intelligence, charisma, willpower, perception, agility). They represent what the creature IS or CAN DO.
> **Do NOT include relationship/social metrics** (e.g., affinity, favorability, bond, intimacy, trust) — these are already handled by the built-in **Relationship** component (value field). Do NOT duplicate them as attribute fields.

\`\`\`typescript
Array<{
  field_name: string,    // Attribute key name (English snake_case)
  hint: string,          // MUST include: (1) what this attribute means, (2) value range (e.g. 0-100), (3) what low/high values imply in-game
  field_display_name?: string  // UI display name (user's language)
}>
\`\`\`

> **hint quality requirements**: The \`hint\` field is the AI narrative engine's ONLY reference for interpreting attribute values at runtime. A vague hint like "strength" is useless — the AI won't know if 30 is strong or weak, or what gameplay effects it should produce.
>
> **Good hint example**: \`"Physical power and raw muscle strength. Range 0-100. Below 20: frail, struggles with basic physical tasks. 40-60: average adult. Above 80: exceptional, can overpower most opponents in melee."\`
>
> **Bad hint example**: \`"How strong the character is"\` — missing range, missing narrative mapping.

#### 2. GameTime — Initial Game Time
\`\`\`typescript
{ year: number, month: number, day: number, hour: number, minute: number }
\`\`\`

#### 3. CustomComponentRegistry — Custom Component Registry

> **Do not duplicate built-in components!** The engine has built-in: Inventory, Relationship, StatusEffects, Creature, LocationRef.

\`\`\`typescript
{
  custom_components: Array<{
    component_key: string,
    component_name: string,
    fields: Array<{
      field_name: string,        // Field key name (English snake_case)
      field_type: string,        // Type descriptor (see rules below)
      field_description: string  // MUST include: what the field tracks, valid values/range, and what changes mean narratively
    }>
  }>
}
\`\`\`

> **When to create a CustomComponent vs. NOT:**
>
> ✅ **Create** when a game mechanic requires **structured, multi-field data that changes over time** and is NOT covered by built-in components. Good examples:
> - A sanity/corruption system with multiple interacting values (level, threshold, triggers)
> - An investigation/clue board tracking discovered evidence and deductions
> - A faction reputation tracker with multiple factions and standing tiers
> - A magic/skill system with learned abilities, cooldowns, resource costs
> - A quest/mission log with structured progress tracking
>
> ❌ **Do NOT create** when:
> - A simple numeric value suffices → use **creature_attr_fields** instead (e.g., a single "sanity" score)
> - It's just a list of strings or tags → use **StatusEffects** with descriptive remarks
> - It's inventory/items → use the built-in **Inventory**
> - It duplicates the exact schema of a built-in component
>
> **Rule of thumb**: If the data has **only 1 field**, it probably belongs in attrs or StatusEffects, not a CustomComponent. CustomComponents shine when you need **2+ interrelated fields** forming a coherent subsystem.
>
> **Every CustomComponent you register MUST have a corresponding "[System Name] System Rules" document** in the documents array. The document must explain what triggers data changes, how each field evolves, and how the AI narrative engine should reflect these in the story. A component without a rules document is dead weight.

> **field_type rules** (max 2 levels of nesting):
> - Primitives: \`"string"\`, \`"number"\`, \`"boolean"\`
> - Arrays of primitives: \`"array<string>"\`, \`"array<number>"\`
> - Nested object: \`"object<{key1:string,key2:number}>"\`
> - Array of objects: \`"array<object<{key1:string,key2:number}>>"\`
>
> Examples:
> - \`{ field_name: "sanity_value", field_type: "number" }\`
> - \`{ field_name: "known_spells", field_type: "array<string>" }\`
> - \`{ field_name: "stats", field_type: "object<{attack:number,defense:number}>" }\`
> - \`{ field_name: "quest_log", field_type: "array<object<{quest_id:string,status:string,progress:number}>>" }\`

#### 4. documents — World-Level Setting Documents (BindSetting)

These documents will be bound to the World entity's BindSetting.documents, serving as foundational rules for the AI narrative engine.

> **Document quality requirements**: Setting documents are the core reference materials for the game's narrative engine and **must be detailed, high-quality long texts**.
> - **Each document must be at least 500 words**; important documents (foundational settings, narrative style) should reach **800-1500 words**.
> - **It is strictly forbidden to generate short documents with empty content**. If a topic truly has limited content, merge it with a related topic.

**Required document types:**
- **Foundational Setting** — Core worldview setting (800-1500 words)
- **World Progression & Story Tone** — A standalone guide for the AI narrative engine on **how to drive the story forward** (600-1000 words). Must include:
  - **Core tone**: What kind of story is this? (e.g., heartwarming, dark, comedic, bittersweet, suspenseful)
  - **Emotional palette**: What emotions should the narrative evoke? What is the overall mood arc?
  - **Encouraged elements**: What themes, events, and narrative beats are welcome? (e.g., slice-of-life moments, moral dilemmas, power struggles, romance, mystery reveals)
  - **Discouraged/forbidden elements**: What should the AI avoid or never include? (e.g., gratuitous violence, deus ex machina, breaking the fourth wall, character deaths without buildup)
  - **Pacing guidance**: How should tension escalate? Should the story be slow-burn or fast-paced? How often should major events occur?
  - **World evolution rules**: How does the world change over time? Are there seasonal cycles, political shifts, escalating threats?
- **Narrative Style** — Writing style guide (800-1500 words)
- **Attribute System Rules** — **MUST cover EVERY creature_attr_field you defined** (500-1000 words). For each field, specify: value range, what each tier means narratively, and how it affects gameplay (e.g., skill checks, dialogue options, combat outcomes). A field without rules in this document is a dead field — the AI narrative engine won't know how to use it.
- **[System Name] System Rules** — **One document per CustomComponent** you registered. Each document must explain: what the system does, how each field works, what triggers changes, and how the AI should narrate effects. **If you create a CustomComponent but don't write its rules document, the AI narrative engine will ignore it at runtime.**

**Narrative style document requirements:**
- Must include: narrative perspective and person, dialogue style, scene description density, taboos and boundaries

> **CRITICAL consistency rule**: Every data definition (attr field, custom component) MUST have corresponding rules in the documents. The pattern is:
> 1. Define the structure (creature_attr_fields / CustomComponentRegistry)
> 2. Write the rules (documents explaining how the AI should use the data)
> If step 2 is missing, the data is orphaned and useless at runtime.

**Document structure:**
\`\`\`typescript
{
  name: string,       // Document name
  content: string,    // Markdown format content (500-1500 words, important documents 800+)
  condition?: string  // RAG recall condition
}
\`\`\`

> **Formatting rule**: Document \`content\` MUST use proper Markdown formatting with **line breaks** (\`\\n\`). Use headings (\`##\`, \`###\`), bullet lists, and paragraph breaks to organize content into clearly separated sections. **Never output a document as a single giant paragraph** — the content will be displayed in an editor and needs to be human-readable and easy to edit.
`,

    // ===== REGIONS =====
    regions: `
## Current Step: Region Design

Based on the region list from the draft, design the game world's regions.

### Temporal Anchoring Principle

The opening story was determined in previous steps. The regions you design should reflect the state **at the story's beginning**.

### / Common Mistakes

\`\`\`diff
  // Path is not simple from/to!
- paths: [{ from: "loc_a", to: "loc_b", description: "..." }]
+ paths: [{
+   src_location: "loc_a", src_region: "region_north",
+   discovered: true,
+   to_region: "region_south", to_location: "loc_b",
+   description: "A path through the valley"
+ }]
\`\`\`

### Design Principles
- Region count should reference the region list from the draft
- Each region needs at least **3 or more locations** to be worth being an independent region
- Locations **within the same region** are connected by paths (src_region and to_region are the same)
- **Cross-region** paths connect different regions
- Ensure the entire map is **connected**

### Granularity Control
- **Don't over-split**: If an area can only be split into 2 locations, merge it into an adjacent region.
- Each region should have sufficient narrative density and explorability.

### BindSetting — Region Setting Documents

Each region generates **at most 1** comprehensive setting document (condition: "When the story takes place in [region name]").

> Only generate documents for regions with unique narrative value.

**Document writing principles:**
- Scene description instruction for the LLM, providing atmosphere and detail references
- Single document **400-800 words**
- Focus on the region's unique sensory details and narrative value
- **Use Markdown formatting with line breaks** — headings, bullet lists, paragraph breaks. Never write as a single block of text.
`,

    // ===== ORGANIZATIONS =====
    organizations: `
## Current Step: Organization Design

Based on the organization list from the draft and the already-generated region data, design the game world's organizations and factions.

### Temporal Anchoring Principle

The opening story was determined in previous steps. The organizations you design should reflect the state **at the story's beginning**.

### Design Principles
- Organizations should correspond to the organization list from the draft
- **region_id and location_id in territories must reference valid IDs generated in the previous step**
- Consider inter-organization relationships (opposition, alliance, neutral, etc.), reflected in the description
- Each organization's organization_id will be referenced by characters (Creature.organization_id)

### BindSetting — Organization Setting Documents

Each organization generates **at most 1** comprehensive setting document (condition: "When the story involves [organization name]").

> Only generate documents for organizations with significant narrative influence.

**Document writing principles:**
- Narrative reference for the LLM, helping AI maintain consistency when role-playing organization members
- Single document **400-800 words**
- Focus on the organization's core ideology, operational style, and external attitudes
- **Use Markdown formatting with line breaks** — headings, bullet lists, paragraph breaks. Never write as a single block of text.
`,

    // ===== CREATURES (parametrized per batch) =====
    creatures: `
## Current Step: Character Design

Based on the character list from the draft, design character data for this batch.

> This step generates characters in batches (up to 3 per batch), each character including complete game data and setting documents.

### Temporal Anchoring Principle (Most Important!)

**All character data** you design must strictly reflect the state **at the story's beginning**, not the state from later developments.

- **Attributes/Stats**: Reflect the character's ability level at the story's opening
- **Relationships**: Reflect relationship states at the story's opening. Characters who haven't met yet **should not have affinity scores**
- **Items/Status**: Only set items and status effects the character actually possesses at the story's opening
- **Future plot clues**: Write into **setting documents (BindSetting)** as foreshadowing, not as current values

### / Common Mistakes

\`\`\`diff
  // known_infos is what the character THEMSELVES know, not what others know about them!
- known_infos: ["Xiao Ming is a brave warrior"]
+ known_infos: ["Unusual monster activity at the northern border", "The city lord is secretly recruiting mercenaries"]

  // attrs are universal — NPCs must fill them too!
- Creature: { creature_id: "npc_01", name: "Xiao Ming", ... }  // ← no attrs
+ Creature: { creature_id: "npc_01", name: "Xiao Ming", ..., attrs: { strength: 60, intelligence: 75, ... } }

  // body MUST include physical trait descriptions!
- appearance: { body: "tall build", clothing: "black combat outfit" }
+ appearance: { body: "Tall and upright frame, broad shoulders, defined muscle lines", clothing: "black combat outfit" }

  // IsPlayer is an empty object {}, not true! NPCs should not have IsPlayer
- IsPlayer: true
+ IsPlayer: {}      // Player character only

  // Inventory items need complete information!
- Inventory: { items: [{ id: "sword", count: 1 }] }
+ Inventory: { items: [{ id: "sword", count: 1, name: "Heirloom Sword", description: "An ancient sword", details: ["Family crest engraved"] }] }

  // titles must be string[]
+ titles: ["Former Dynasty Orphan", "Northern Guardian"]

  // CustomComponents can only use registered component_keys!
- CustomComponents: { custom_components: [{ component_key: "my_new_system", data: { ... } }] }
+ CustomComponents: { custom_components: [{ component_key: "sanity", data: { sanity_value: 80, panic_level: "calm" } }] }

  // StatusEffects: remark describes MEANING & RULES, data stores ACTUAL VALUES
  // ❌ remark must NOT contain current numeric values — those belong in data!
- status_effects: [{ instance_id: "curse", remark: "Cursed", data: { level: 3, decay: 0.5 } }]
- status_effects: [{ instance_id: "curse", remark: "level(severity)=3(max 5), decay(daily)=0.5", data: { level: 3, decay: 0.5 } }]
+ status_effects: [{ instance_id: "shadow_curse", display_name: "Shadow Curse",
+   remark: "Afflicted by shadow curse. data fields: level = severity (0-5, higher is worse); decay = daily natural decay rate",
+   data: { level: 3, decay: 0.5 }
+ }]
\`\`\`

### Design Principles
- **All characters listed in this batch must be generated**, none may be omitted
- **Exactly one character** (the player character) has \`IsPlayer: {}\`; all others are NPCs
- **gender is required and must be specific** (e.g., "male"/"female"), not "non-binary" or "unknown"
- **appearance.body must include physical trait descriptions** consistent with gender
- attrs keys must match the creature_attr_fields defined in world_data
- LocationRef must reference valid region_id / location_id
- CustomComponents component_key must be registered in CustomComponentRegistry

### BindSetting — Character Setting Documents

> Core characters: 1-2 documents; secondary characters: 1 document; background characters: may not need documents.

**Document writing principles:**
- Core reference material for AI to understand and portray characters
- Core character basic setting documents should be **800+ words**, secondary characters **500+ words**
- **Maximum 2 documents per character**
- **Use Markdown formatting with line breaks** — headings, bullet lists, paragraph breaks. Never write as a single block of text.
`,

    // ===== INITIAL STORY =====
    initial_story: `
## Current Step: Opening Story Construction

Based on the gameplay tone, opening timing, and storyline from the draft, create a brief opening text.

> The opening story will serve as the **temporal anchor** for all subsequent steps.

### Data to Generate

\`\`\`typescript
{
  background: string,  // Background narration. Briefly establish "who I am and why I'm here". (100-200 words)
  start_story: string  // Opening first act. An immersive scene description or dialogue. (200-500 words)
}
\`\`\`

### Writing Principles
- **Brief and refined**
- **Absolutely do not provide options/choices**
- **Reject chronological recaps and setting info-dumps**
- Style must match the gameplay tone described in the draft
`,
}

// ============================================================================
// Extraction Prompt Templates (for sub-agent pre-extraction)
// ============================================================================

/**
 * Build a sub-agent extraction prompt for a given phase.
 * The sub-agent reads the full file/lorebook content and extracts phase-relevant info.
 */
export function getExtractionPrompt(
    phaseId: WBNPhaseId,
    draft?: WBNDraftOutput,
    openingStory?: { background?: string; start_story?: string },
    initialPrompt?: string,
    userDesignNotes?: string,
): string {
    // Common context header
    const contextParts: string[] = []
    if (initialPrompt) {
        contextParts.push(`## User's Original Request\n\n${initialPrompt}`)
    }
    if (userDesignNotes) {
        contextParts.push(`## User's Design Preferences (Questionnaire Answers)\n\n${userDesignNotes}`)
    }
    if (draft) {
        contextParts.push(`## Draft Summary\n- **Tone**: ${draft.tone}\n- **Opening**: ${draft.opening}\n- **Storyline**: ${draft.storyline}\n- **Mechanics**: ${draft.mechanics}\n- **Protagonist**: ${draft.protagonist}`)
        if (draft.regions?.length) {
            contextParts.push(`- **Regions**: ${draft.regions.map(r => r.name).join(', ')}`)
        }
        if (draft.organizations?.length) {
            contextParts.push(`- **Organizations**: ${draft.organizations.map(o => o.name).join(', ')}`)
        }
        if (draft.creatures?.length) {
            contextParts.push(`- **Characters**: ${draft.creatures.map(c => `${c.name}${c.is_player ? ' (player)' : ''}`).join(', ')}`)
        }
    }
    if (openingStory?.start_story) {
        const preview = openingStory.start_story.length > 300
            ? openingStory.start_story.substring(0, 300) + '...'
            : openingStory.start_story
        contextParts.push(`## Opening Story (temporal anchor)\n${preview}`)
    }
    const contextBlock = contextParts.length > 0 ? contextParts.join('\n\n') + '\n\n---\n\n' : ''

    // Quality requirements appended to every phase extraction prompt
    const EXTRACTION_QUALITY_RULES = `

---

## Extraction Quality Requirements (MANDATORY)

Your job is to be a **thorough, detail-preserving information extractor** — NOT a summarizer. The downstream generator has no access to the original material; your output is its ONLY window into the source. Any detail you omit is permanently lost.

### Output Structure

For each topic/entity, your output must have **three layers**:

1. **Overview** — A 2-3 sentence summary of the topic/entity's role and significance.
2. **Detailed Breakdown** — Exhaustive bullet points covering every relevant detail found in the source. Include specific names, numbers, descriptions, rules, and nuances. Do NOT generalize or condense — if the source says "her eyes are amber with golden flecks that glow faintly in moonlight", write exactly that, not "golden eyes".
3. **Source Quotes** — For every significant detail, include a direct quote from the original material in a blockquote (\`>\`) with enough context to be useful. This preserves the original author's voice, phrasing, and intent.

### Rules

- **Exhaustiveness over brevity**: Extract EVERYTHING relevant, even minor details. A 3000-word extraction is better than a 500-word summary if the source warrants it.
- **Preserve specificity**: Keep exact numbers, proper nouns, specific descriptions, unique phrasings. Never replace specific details with vague generalizations.
- **Preserve the author's voice**: When the source material has distinctive descriptions, metaphors, or characterizations, quote them directly rather than paraphrasing.
- **Do NOT invent or infer**: Only extract what is explicitly stated or strongly implied. Flag any inferences clearly as "[inferred]".
- **When in doubt, include it**: If you're unsure whether a detail is relevant to this phase, include it anyway. Redundancy is acceptable; information loss is not.`

    const prompts: Record<WBNPhaseId, string> = {
        synopsis: `${contextBlock}## Task
Read all provided material and extract a comprehensive overview:
1. All character names, their roles, relationships, and brief descriptions
2. All location/region names and their descriptions
3. All organization/faction names and their descriptions
4. World-building details: magic systems, technology level, social structure, time period
5. Key plot points, conflicts, and story arcs
6. Any gameplay-related details (if present)

Output a well-structured extraction covering all the above. Be thorough — this overview will guide the entire world-building process.${EXTRACTION_QUALITY_RULES}`,

        initial_story: `${contextBlock}## Task
Extract information relevant to the **opening scene** of the story:
1. The time, place, and circumstances of the story's beginning
2. The protagonist's initial situation and emotional state
3. Atmospheric details: weather, environment, sensory descriptions
4. Any specific opening scene descriptions in the material
5. Key events or encounters that kick off the story

Focus on details that help write an immersive opening — atmosphere, sensory details, emotional tone. Extract vivid descriptions and distinctive phrasings from the source.${EXTRACTION_QUALITY_RULES}`,

        world_data: `${contextBlock}## Task
Extract information relevant to world-level systems and settings:
1. Magic/power/technology systems and their rules
2. Social structures, governance, economy
3. Historical background and timeline
4. Any attribute/stat systems described
5. Custom gameplay mechanics (investigation, crafting, etc.)
6. World rules, taboos, cultural norms
7. Time period and calendar details

Focus on systemic information that defines how the world works, not individual characters or locations. Include all specific rules, mechanics, and constraints mentioned.${EXTRACTION_QUALITY_RULES}`,

        regions: `${contextBlock}## Task
Extract all geographical and location information:
${draft?.regions?.length ? `Focus especially on these regions: ${draft.regions.map(r => `**${r.name}** (${r.region_id})`).join(', ')}\n` : ''}
1. Region/area descriptions: geography, climate, atmosphere
2. Specific locations within regions: buildings, landmarks, natural features
3. Connections between locations and regions (paths, roads, routes)
4. Cultural and environmental characteristics of each area
5. Any notable features, dangers, or resources

For each region, provide exhaustive detail about its locations, atmosphere, and sensory characteristics. Quote vivid environmental descriptions from the source.${EXTRACTION_QUALITY_RULES}`,

        organizations: `${contextBlock}## Task
Extract all organization and faction information:
${draft?.organizations?.length ? `Focus especially on these organizations: ${draft.organizations.map(o => `**${o.name}** (${o.organization_id})`).join(', ')}\n` : ''}
1. Organization names, purposes, and ideologies
2. Internal structures and hierarchies
3. Territories and bases of operation
4. Inter-faction relationships (alliances, rivalries, neutral)
5. Key figures and their roles within organizations
6. Recruitment, membership, and operational methods

Provide exhaustive details about each organization's character, internal dynamics, and role in the world.${EXTRACTION_QUALITY_RULES}`,

        creatures: `${contextBlock}## Task
Extract character information for the following characters, **focusing on their state at the story's beginning** (not later developments):
${draft?.creatures?.length ? draft.creatures.map(c => `- **${c.name}** (${c.creature_id})${c.is_player ? ' — PLAYER CHARACTER' : ''}`).join('\n') : '(see batch list in instructions)'}

For each character, extract:
1. **Personality**: traits, temperament, speech patterns, verbal tics, behavioral habits
2. **Appearance**: physical features, build, height, hair, eyes, skin, distinguishing marks, clothing
3. **Background**: history, origin, family, experiences before story begins
4. **Abilities**: skills, powers, expertise, fighting style
5. **Relationships**: connections to other characters AT THE STORY'S BEGINNING
6. **Current state**: emotional state, goals, situation at story opening
7. **Known information**: what the character themselves know (intelligence, secrets)
8. **Items**: possessions, equipment, artifacts

**CRITICAL**: Distinguish between "state at story's beginning" and "state after later developments". Only report the former for data fields. Note any important future developments separately — these will go into setting documents.${EXTRACTION_QUALITY_RULES}`,
    }

    return prompts[phaseId]
}

// ============================================================================
// Game Style Supplements (injected into phase instructions)
// ============================================================================

const GAME_STYLE_WORLD_DATA: Record<WBNGameStyle, string> = {
    numerical: `
### Game Style: Numerical / Stats-Driven

This game uses **precise numeric attributes** to drive gameplay. Design data accordingly:

- **creature_attr_fields**: Design **5-10 numeric attribute fields** with clear value ranges (e.g., 0-100). Include combat stats, social stats, or skill levels as appropriate for the genre. Each field should meaningfully affect gameplay outcomes.
- **CustomComponentRegistry**: Design **1-3 custom components** for core game systems that need **multi-field structured tracking** (e.g., a skill tree with skill entries, a crafting system with recipes and materials, a combat stance system with modes and modifiers). Each component should represent a coherent subsystem, not a single value. If a mechanic only needs one number, put it in creature_attr_fields instead.
- **Documents**: The "Attribute System Rules" document must detail how each numeric value maps to in-game effects (e.g., "strength 80+ can break barriers", "charisma below 30 causes NPCs to be dismissive").
`,
    narrative: `
### Game Style: Narrative / Story-Driven

This game prioritizes **narrative experience over numerical mechanics**. Design data accordingly:

- **creature_attr_fields**: Use **at most 2-4 qualitative or abstract fields** that represent the creature's **intrinsic traits or abilities** (e.g., \`resolve\`, \`intuition\`, \`sanity\`, \`eloquence\`). These should be simple indicators that drive story branching, not min-maxable stats. Numeric ranges should be small (e.g., 0-10) or use descriptive tiers.
- **CustomComponentRegistry**: **Avoid creating custom components unless the narrative truly demands a multi-field tracking system** (e.g., an investigation clue board with evidence + deduction fields, a psychological profile with multiple interacting dimensions). A single-value tracker (e.g., just a "sanity score") should be a creature_attr_field, not a CustomComponent. If no custom component is needed, output an empty array: \`{ "custom_components": [] }\`.
- **Documents**: Focus the "Attribute System Rules" document on how attribute changes reflect **character development arcs and narrative turning points**, not mechanical bonuses. The narrative style document is especially important — invest more detail here.
`,
}

const GAME_STYLE_CREATURES: Record<WBNGameStyle, string> = {
    numerical: `
### Game Style: Numerical / Stats-Driven

- **attrs**: Fill all defined attribute fields with **meaningful numeric values** that differentiate characters. Consider power balance across the cast.
- **Inventory**: Give characters **items with gameplay-relevant properties** (weapons, consumables, key items with stat effects).
- **StatusEffects**: Store all numeric values in the \`data\` object (e.g., \`{ level: 3, duration: 5, decay: 0.5 }\`). The \`remark\` should only explain what each data field **means** and its value range (e.g., "level = severity 0-5; decay = daily natural recovery rate") — never repeat the actual numbers in the remark.
- **CustomComponents**: Fill in all registered custom component data with appropriate initial values.
`,
    narrative: `
### Game Style: Narrative / Story-Driven

- **attrs**: Use the few defined attribute fields as **narrative indicators**. Values should reflect the character's story role, not combat power (e.g., a traumatized character might have low \`resolve\`, a beloved leader has high \`reputation\`).
- **Inventory**: Only give characters **narratively significant items** — keepsakes, plot devices, symbolic objects. Avoid generic RPG gear (potions, +1 swords). Each item's \`description\` and \`details\` should hint at backstory or plot hooks.
- **StatusEffects**: Use sparingly and only for **narratively active conditions** (e.g., a curse that drives the plot, a disguise). Keep \`data\` simple — a single field or even empty \`{}\` is fine. The \`remark\` should describe the narrative significance and what each data field means — never put actual values in the remark.
- **CustomComponents**: Only fill if custom components were defined. Keep data minimal and story-relevant.
`,
}

const GAME_STYLE_SYNOPSIS: Record<WBNGameStyle, string> = {
    numerical: `
### Game Style: Numerical / Stats-Driven

When generating the **mechanics** field, describe concrete gameplay systems: attribute checks, combat mechanics, resource management, progression systems, etc. The game should feel like a structured RPG with clear rules.
`,
    narrative: `
### Game Style: Narrative / Story-Driven

When generating the **mechanics** field, focus on narrative interaction: dialogue choices, relationship dynamics, exploration discovery, moral dilemmas, branching consequences. Avoid describing numerical combat or stat-check systems. The game should feel like an interactive novel or visual novel.
`,
}

// ============================================================================
// Public API
// ============================================================================

export function getPhaseSystemPrompt(): string {
    return UNIFIED_SYSTEM_PROMPT
}

export function getPhaseUserInstruction(
    phaseId: WBNPhaseId,
    gameStyle?: WBNGameStyle,
    options?: { skipOrganizations?: boolean }
): string {
    let instruction = PHASE_INSTRUCTIONS[phaseId]

    if (gameStyle) {
        if (phaseId === 'synopsis' && GAME_STYLE_SYNOPSIS[gameStyle]) {
            instruction += GAME_STYLE_SYNOPSIS[gameStyle]
        }
        if (phaseId === 'world_data' && GAME_STYLE_WORLD_DATA[gameStyle]) {
            instruction += GAME_STYLE_WORLD_DATA[gameStyle]
        }
        if (phaseId === 'creatures' && GAME_STYLE_CREATURES[gameStyle]) {
            instruction += GAME_STYLE_CREATURES[gameStyle]
        }
    }

    if (options?.skipOrganizations && phaseId === 'synopsis') {
        instruction += `\n\n### Organizations Phase Skipped\n\nThe user has chosen to **skip the dedicated organizations generation phase**. You may still list organizations/factions in the \`organizations\` field if they are naturally relevant to the story — these names will be used as reference in the world_data phase. If the world genuinely has no notable organizations, output an empty array.\n`
    }

    if (options?.skipOrganizations && phaseId === 'world_data') {
        instruction += `\n\n### Organizations Overview (if applicable)\n\nThe dedicated organizations phase is skipped. If the draft includes organizations that are relevant to the narrative, include an additional document in the \`documents\` array:\n- **Name**: "Organizations & Factions Overview" (in user's language)\n- **Content**: For each organization, briefly cover: purpose/ideology, key figures, sphere of influence, and inter-organization relationships. (**400-800 words**)\n- **Condition**: "When the story involves any organization or faction"\n\nIf the world has no meaningful organizations or factions, simply skip this document — it is not required.\n`
    }

    return instruction
}

// ============================================================================
// Synopsis Phase Instruction (for query_user step)
// ============================================================================

export const SYNOPSIS_QUERY_INSTRUCTION = `
## Current Step: Draft — Collect Design Preferences

Based on the user's description and any reference material summary provided, you need to collect the user's design preferences before generating the draft.

Call the \`query_user\` tool to ask the user **5-7 questions**, comprehensively collecting key information about worldview and game design.

**The only situation where questions can be skipped:** The user's initial description has **explicitly and individually** covered all of the following dimensions: story tone, protagonist setup, gameplay direction, core themes, narrative structure, opening timing. In this case, do NOT call query_user — instead just respond with text saying you have enough information.

**Question rules:**
- **Call \`query_user\` only once**, combining all questions into the same form.
- Questions should be **tailored to the user's initial description and reference material**.
- Each question **must** provide \`options\` (option list), with type using \`select\` or \`multiselect\`.
- Questions should cover:
  1. **Story Tone** — Overall atmosphere and narrative style
  2. **Protagonist Positioning** — Protagonist's identity, stance, personality direction
  3. **Gameplay Focus** — Emphasis on social/exploration/combat/puzzle-solving, etc.
  4. **Core Themes** — Themes or conflicts to explore
  5. **Narrative Structure** — Linear/branching/open-ended, etc.
  6. **Opening Timing** — At what point and scene does the story begin?
`

    const schema = schemas[phaseId]
    if (!schema) {
        throw new Error(`No structured output schema for phase: ${phaseId}`)
    }
    return schema
}

/src/games/CUSTOM_GAME_GUIDE.md:
# 🎮 Custom Game Frontend Development Guide > 本指南面向希望基于本框架构建自定义 AI 游戏前端的开发者。框架内置的 Ink 游戏（互动小说）只是一个参考实现——你可以用同一套后端服务做出各种类型的 AI 游戏。 ## 目录 - [架构总览](#架构总览) - [快速开始](#快速开始) - [共享服务 API 参考](#共享服务-api-参考) - [核心概念：CreativeWriting 与 output_content_schema](#核心概念creativewriting-与-output_content_schema) - [游戏类型灵感](#游戏类型灵感) - [参考：Ink 游戏如何使用这些服务](#参考ink-游戏如何使用这些服务) --- ## 架构总览 ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ App.tsx │ │ publish_type → 'INK' | 'CUSTOM' | 'TEST' | ... │ │ ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────────┐ │ │ │ InkGame │ │CustomGame │ │ YourGame │ │ │ │ (参考实现) │ │ (空白模板) │ │ (你的作品!) │ │ │ └─────┬─────┘ └─────┬─────┘ └───────┬───────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ └──────────────┼────────────────┘ │ │ ▼ │ │ games/utils/gameServices.ts │ │ (共享服务 API — 统一入口) │ │ │ │ │ ▼ │ │ window.callService() │ │ (Lua 后端 RPC 桥接) │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ▼ ┌──────────────────┐ │ Lua ECS Backend │ │ ├── 世界状态 │ │ ├── 实体管理 │ │ ├── RAG 召回 │ │ └── LLM 生成 │ └──────────────────┘ ``` **关键认识：** 你的游戏前端只负责 **UI 和交互逻辑**。所有 AI 生成、状态持久化、存档管理都由后端服务完成，你只需调用 `games/utils/gameServices.ts` 中的函数即可。 --- ## 快速开始 ### 1. 创建你的游戏组件 在 `front/src/games/custom/index.tsx`（或新建一个目录）中创建你的游戏组件： ```tsx import { useState, useEffect } from 'react' import { getGameState, getPlayerEntity, creativeWritingStream, createSave, updateGameStateAndDocs, } from '../utils' import type { CreativeWritingOutput } from '../../api/types' interface MyGameProps { onBack: () => void } export default function MyGame({ onBack }: MyGameProps) { const [player, setPlayer] = useState(null) const [content, setContent] = useState('') const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false) // 初始化：加载玩家数据 useEffect(() => { const init = async () => { const playerData = await getPlayerEntity() if (playerData.found) { setPlayer(playerData) } } init() }, []) // AI 生成 const handleGenerate = async (userAction: string) => { setLoading(true) setContent('') await creativeWritingStream({ create_request: `玩家行动: ${userAction}`, thinking_instruction: '分析玩家行动的合理性和后果', previous_content_overview: content, output_content_schema: `{ narrative: string, // 叙事内容 choices: Array<{ // 下一步选择 text: string, difficulty?: number }> }`, callback: (event) => { if (event.event_type === 'result_update' && event.event_data.content) { setContent(event.event_data.content.narrative || '') } if (event.event_type === 'done') { setLoading(false) } if (event.event_type === 'error') { console.error('生成出错:', event.event_data) setLoading(false) } } }) } return (

我的 AI 游戏

{player &&

玩家: {player.Creature?.name}

}
{content}
) } ``` ### 2. 注册你的游戏 在 `App.tsx` 中，你的游戏通过 `publish_type` 路由： ```tsx // App.tsx 中已有的路由逻辑 type GameType = 'NOVEL' | 'INK' | 'TEST' | 'CUSTOM' const renderGame = () => { switch (activeGame) { case 'INK': return {}} /> case 'CUSTOM': return {}} /> // ← 你的游戏 // 你也可以添加更多 GameType... } } ``` 在编辑器的 AppInfo 中将 `publish_type` 设为 `CUSTOM`，即可在启动时加载你的游戏。 ### 3. 使用 Zustand 管理状态（推荐） 对于复杂游戏，建议参考 Ink 游戏的做法，创建 Zustand store 来管理状态： ```tsx // stores/myGameStore.ts import { create } from 'zustand' import { getGameState, creativeWritingStream, createSave } from '../../utils' interface MyGameState { // 你的游戏状态 initialized: boolean // 你的 actions initialize: () => Promise } export const useMyGameStore = create((set, get) => ({ initialized: false, initialize: async () => { const state = await getGameState() if (state.success && state.data) { // 从 state.data 中提取你需要的初始数据 set({ initialized: true }) } }, })) ``` --- ## 共享服务 API 参考 所有服务函数位于 `games/utils/gameServices.ts`，通过 `games/utils/index.ts` 导出。 ### 状态管理 | 函数 | 说明 | 返回类型 | |------|------|----------| | `getGameState()` | 获取完整游戏状态快照 | `GetGameStateOutput` | | `loadState(data)` | 将完整状态写回引擎 | `LoadGameStateOutput` | `StateData` 包含所有游戏数据：世界信息、角色、地域、组织、设定文档、剧情历史等。 ### 存档管理 | 函数 | 说明 | 返回类型 | |------|------|----------| | `createSave(opts?)` | 创建存档点 | `CreateGameSaveOutput` | | `loadSave(checkpointId)` | 加载指定存档 | `LoadGameSaveOutput` | | `listSaves()` | 列出所有存档 | `ListGameSavesOutput` | ### 剧情历史 | 函数 | 说明 | 返回类型 | |------|------|----------| | `getStoryHistory()` | 获取完整剧情历史 | `GetStoryHistoryOutput` | | `setNewStoryHistory(input)` | 添加新的历史条目 | `void` | | `clearStoryHistory()` | 清空所有历史 | `ClearStoryHistoryOutput` | 你可以自由定义 `content` 的数据格式——它就是一个 `any` 类型，由你的游戏负责序列化和反序列化。 ### AI 内容生成 | 函数 | 说明 | 返回类型 | |------|------|----------| | `creativeWritingStream(input)` | 流式 AI 内容生成 | `void` | | `updateGameStateAndDocs(input)` | 根据叙事事件更新游戏状态 | `UpdateGameStateAndDocsOutput` | ### 实体查询（ECS） | 函数 | 说明 | 返回类型 | |------|------|----------| | `getPlayerEntity()` | 查询玩家实体 | `PlayerEntityOutput` | | `getNPCEntities()` | 查询所有 NPC | `NPCEntitiesOutput` | ### 发布与配置 | 函数 | 说明 | |------|------| | `initializeGame()` | 初始化游戏模板 | | `getAppInfo()` | 获取应用信息 | | `publishApp()` | 发布应用 | | `publishArticle(input)` | 发布文章 | | `setAPIConfig(input)` | 配置 LLM 模型 | --- ## 核心概念：CreativeWriting 与 output_content_schema `creativeWritingStream` 是整个框架的**核心引擎**。它不仅仅是"写小说"——它是一个通用的 **RAG 增强 + 流式 LLM 生成** 管线，你可以通过 `output_content_schema` 让 AI 输出任何结构化数据。 ### CreativeWritingStreamInput 详解 ```typescript interface CreativeWritingStreamInput { create_request: string // 告诉 AI "要做什么" thinking_instruction: string // 告诉 AI "如何思考" previous_content_overview: string // 之前的上下文摘要 output_content_schema: string // 输出数据的 TypeScript 格式定义 callback: (event) => void // 流式事件回调 model?: string // 可选：指定模型 } ``` ### output_content_schema 的威力 这是一个**字符串形式的 TypeScript 接口定义**，AI 会按照你定义的结构返回数据。这意味着你可以让 AI 输出任何格式的结构化数据： ```typescript // 小说模式（Ink 游戏的做法） output_content_schema: `{ chapter_hint: string, novel_content_part1: string, novel_content_part2: string, player_choices: Array<{ name: string, description: string }>, state_changes: string[], setting_changes: string[] }` // 战斗系统 output_content_schema: `{ battle_narration: string, damage_dealt: number, effects_applied: Array<{ target: string, effect: string, duration: number }>, enemy_action: { type: string, description: string }, battle_state: 'ongoing' | 'victory' | 'defeat' }` // 对话系统 output_content_schema: `{ speaker: string, dialogue: string, emotion: string, relationship_change: number, available_responses: Array<{ text: string, tone: string }> }` // 探索/解谜 output_content_schema: `{ scene_description: string, discovered_items: Array<{ id: string, name: string, description: string }>, available_actions: string[], hidden_clue?: string }` ``` ### 流式回调事件 回调函数会收到以下事件类型： ```typescript type StreamEvent = { event_type: 'collector_result_update' | 'result_update' | 'done' | 'error' event_data: Partial | Error } ``` | 事件类型 | 触发时机 | event_data 内容 | |----------|----------|-----------------| | `collector_result_update` | RAG 召回完成 | `collector_results`: 被召回的实体和文档列表 | | `result_update` | AI 输出更新（流式） | `content`: 你定义的结构化数据（部分或完整） | | `done` | 生成结束 | 最终的完整 `content` | | `error` | 出错 | `Error` 对象 | ### RAG 知识召回 `creativeWritingStream` 的一大强项是内置的 **RAG（检索增强生成）** 机制。AI 在生成之前，会自动根据上下文从设定文档中召回相关知识。你不需要手动管理这些——只需在编辑器中编写好设定文档，AI 就会在合适的时机引用它们。 `collector_result_update` 事件会告诉你哪些实体和文档被召回了，你可以选择在 UI 中展示这些信息（就像 Ink 游戏的 RAG 面板那样），也可以完全隐藏。 ### 状态变更与自动更新 AI 在 `output_content_schema` 中返回 `state_changes` 和 `setting_changes` 时，你可以将它们传递给 `updateGameStateAndDocs` 服务，由后端 AI 自动生成并执行 Lua 代码来更新游戏世界状态： ```typescript // AI 生成完成后 if (result.state_changes?.length || result.setting_changes?.length) { const updateResult = await updateGameStateAndDocs({ new_event: narrativeContent, // 发生了什么 state_changes: result.state_changes || [], // AI 建议的状态变化 setting_changes: result.setting_changes || [] // AI 建议的设定变化 }) if (updateResult.success) { // 刷新前端数据 // ... } } ``` --- ## 游戏类型灵感 以下是一些你可以用这个框架实现的 AI 游戏类型： ### 🎭 互动对话 / 视觉小说 ```typescript output_content_schema: `{ speaker: string, dialogue: string, inner_thought?: string, background_change?: string, character_expression: string, choices: Array<{ text: string, leads_to: string }> }` ``` ### ⚔️ 回合制 RPG 战斗 ```typescript output_content_schema: `{ turn_narration: string, enemy_intent: string, battle_log: Array<{ actor: string, action: string, result: string }>, player_options: Array<{ type: 'attack' | 'skill' | 'item' | 'defend' | 'flee', name: string, description: string, success_rate?: number }> }` ``` ### 🔍 推理解谜 ```typescript output_content_schema: `{ scene: string, dialogue?: { speaker: string, text: string }, evidence_found?: Array<{ id: string, name: string, description: string }>, deduction_prompt?: string, available_actions: Array<{ action: string, target?: string }> }` ``` ### 🏰 经营模拟 ```typescript output_content_schema: `{ day_report: string, resource_changes: Array<{ resource: string, delta: number, reason: string }>, events: Array<{ type: string, description: string, choices: string[] }>, advisor_suggestions: string[] }` ``` ### 🃏 卡牌对战 ```typescript output_content_schema: `{ round_narration: string, opponent_play: { card_name: string, effect: string }, board_state: { player_hp: number, opponent_hp: number }, hand_advice: string, available_plays: Array<{ card: string, mana_cost: number, effect_preview: string }> }` ``` ### 🌍 开放世界探索 ```typescript output_content_schema: `{ location_description: string, atmosphere: string, npcs_present: Array<{ name: string, activity: string, approachable: boolean }>, points_of_interest: Array<{ name: string, hint: string }>, travel_options: Array<{ destination: string, distance: string, risk: string }> }` ``` --- ## 参考：Ink 游戏如何使用这些服务 Ink 游戏是框架内置的参考实现，展示了完整的服务集成模式： ### 初始化流程 ``` 组件挂载 → getGameState() 获取世界数据、背景故事 → getPlayerEntity() 获取玩家实体 → getNPCEntities() 获取所有 NPC → getStoryHistory() 恢复历史记录 ``` ### 每回合游戏循环 ``` 1. 玩家选择行动 2. creativeWritingStream() → AI 生成叙事 + 选择项 + 状态变化建议 ├── collector_result_update → 展示 RAG 召回信息 ├── result_update → 流式展示小说内容 └── done → 生成完成 3. createSave() → 创建临时存档（用于失败回滚） 4. updateGameStateAndDocs() → 后端执行状态变更 5. setNewStoryHistory() → 保存本回合历史 6. createSave() → 创建正式存档 7. 刷新前端数据（重新查询实体） ``` ### 错误恢复机制 Ink 游戏在执行 `updateGameStateAndDocs` 前会创建临时存档，如果状态更新失败，可以通过 `loadSave(tempCheckpointId)` 回滚到更新前的状态。建议你的游戏也实施类似的回滚机制。 --- ## 最佳实践 1. **始终使用 `games/utils` 导入服务** — 不要直接调用 `window.callService` 2. **设计好你的 `output_content_schema`** — 这是你游戏体验的核心，AI 的输出质量与 schema 设计直接相关 3. **利用 `thinking_instruction`** — 引导 AI 的思考方向，产生更合理的内容 4. **实现状态回滚** — 在修改状态前创建存档，失败时可回滚 5. **保存剧情历史** — 使用 `setNewStoryHistory` 持久化每回合数据，支持断点续玩 6. **利用 RAG** — 在编辑器中编写丰富的设定文档，AI 会自动在生成时召回相关内容 7. **流式展示** — 充分利用 `callback` 的流式事件，给用户实时反馈而非长时间等待 --- ## 文件结构建议 ``` front/src/games/ ├── utils/ │ ├── gameServices.ts ← 共享服务 API（已提供） │ └── index.ts ← barrel 导出 ├── components/ ← 共享 UI 组件（如模态框） ├── custom/ ← 你的自定义游戏 │ ├── index.tsx ← 主入口组件 │ ├── stores/ ← Zustand 状态管理 │ │ ├── gameStore.ts │ │ └── uiStore.ts │ ├── components/ ← 游戏专用组件 │ └── MyGame.css ← 游戏样式 └── ink/ ← Ink 参考实现（供学习） ├── index.tsx ├── stores/ ├── components/ └── types.ts ```

# Custom Game Architecture (Visual Novel)

## Directory Structure

```
custom/
├── index.tsx                  # 入口：初始化所有 store，渲染开始画面 / VN 主界面 / 模态层
├── types.ts                   # 类型定义：GalTurn, GalDialogue, GalExpression 等
├── hooks.tsx                  # useHighlightEntities（实体高亮）, useGameRegistries
├── GalgameGame.css            # 全部样式（场景、文本框、选项、抽屉、设置等）
├── stores/
│   ├── index.ts               # 统一导出
│   ├── gameStore.ts           # 核心：回合管理、流式生成、存档、状态更新
│   ├── creatureStore.ts       # 玩家 & NPC 实体、游戏时间、导演笔记
│   ├── registryStore.ts       # 技能/物品/招式/组织/地点 等注册表
│   ├── modalStore.ts          # 各种模态框开关状态
│   ├── spriteStore.ts         # 立绘管理（IndexedDB 持久化）
│   └── uiStore.ts             # UI 偏好（打字速度、自动播放、对话行数）
├── hooks/
│   └── useTypewriter.ts       # 复用 ink 的打字机效果 hook
└── components/
    ├── index.ts               # 统一导出
    ├── GalFlow.tsx            # VNPresenter：场景+文本框+控制栏+选项+日志+设置
    ├── Modals.tsx             # PublishModal：发布章节为文章
    └── SpriteManager.tsx      # 立绘上传/删除/导入导出 UI
```

## Data Flow

### 初始化

```
index.tsx useEffect
  → resetStores()
  → loadInitialData()      // 背景故事
  → loadRegistries()       // 技能、物品、生物等注册表
  → refreshCreatures()     // 玩家 + NPC 实体
  → loadEntityMaps()       // 游戏时间、地区、组织
  → loadStoryHistory()     // 恢复之前的剧情进度
  → initSprites()          // 从 IndexedDB 加载立绘
```

### 回合循环

```
1. 玩家选择/输入 → PlayerActionTurn 加入 galTurns[]
2. generateStory() 流式生成 → GalStoryTurn（含 dialogues[], choices[]）
3. 生成完成 → updateGameStateAndDocs() 更新后端状态
4. 刷新实体数据 → 等待下一次玩家选择
```

### 生成阶段 (currentPhase)

- `idle` — 空闲
- `generating-story` — AI 流式生成中（子阶段：collecting → reasoning → thinking → writing）
- `updating-state` — 将剧情变化同步到后端
- `waiting-choice` — 显示选项，等待玩家操作

## Store 职责

| Store | 职责 | 持久化 |
|-------|------|--------|
| gameStore | 回合历史、生成阶段、玩家输入、错误信息 | 后端 checkpoint |
| creatureStore | 玩家/NPC 实体、世界地图、时间 | 后端 state |
| registryStore | 游戏世界元数据（技能、物品、地点等） | 后端 state |
| modalStore | 模态框开关 + 实体引用 | 无 |
| spriteStore | 角色立绘 (creatureId__expression → dataUrl) | IndexedDB |
| uiStore | 打字速度、自动播放延迟、对话行数 | localStorage |

## 组件层级

```
index.tsx
├── 开始画面（未开始时）
│   └── 背景故事 + START 按钮
├── VN 主界面（开始后）
│   ├── VNPresenter          ← GalFlow.tsx
│   │   ├── 场景区（立绘 + 背景描写）
│   │   ├── 文本框（说话人 + 对话 + 打字机效果）
│   │   ├── 选项覆盖层
│   │   ├── VNLogDrawer      ← 历史日志抽屉
│   │   └── VNSettingsPanel  ← 设置面板
│   └── 导航按钮（返回/角色/世界/立绘/发布）
└── 模态层
    ├── CreaturePanel        ← 玩家角色面板
    ├── CreatureModal        ← NPC 详情
    ├── OrganizationModal    ← 组织详情
    ├── LocationModal        ← 地点详情
    ├── EntryModal           ← 百科词条
    ├── InfoModal            ← 通用信息
    ├── PublishModal         ← 发布文章
    ├── WorldOverview        ← 世界总览
    └── SpriteManager        ← 立绘管理
```

## 关键模式

- **流式生成**：`creativeWritingStream()` 通过回调逐步更新 GalStoryTurn 的 dialogues/choices
- **实体高亮**：`useHighlightEntities()` 扫描对话文本，匹配角色/组织/地点名，生成可点击链接
- **立绘系统**：8 种表情 (normal/happy/angry/sad/surprised/shy/disgusted/dazed)，fallback 到 normal
- **错误恢复**：生成失败时创建临时 checkpoint，可回滚重试
